title: Beige Book
summary: This source is a Federal Reserve navigation page for the Beige Book report, not the actual Beige Book content. It describes the Beige Book as a summary of commentary on current economic conditions gathered from the twelve Federal Reserve Districts. The page lists 2026 publication dates (January 14, March 4, April 15, June 3, July 15, September 2, October 14, November 25) and was last updated March 4, 2026. No actual district-level economic data or commentary is present in this source.
key_points: This is a navigation/landing page, not the actual Beige Book report content
The Beige Book is published eight times per year by the Federal Reserve
Each District report is gathered by the respective Federal Reserve Bank through contacts with business leaders and economists
2026 publication dates listed: January 14, March 4, April 15, June 3, July 15, September 2, October 14, November 25
Last update to the page was March 4, 2026
No actual economic commentary or district-level data is contained in this source
why_it_matters: This page is not archiveable content. The actual Beige Book reports containing district-level economic conditions are not present here. Researchers seeking Beige Book data must access the linked HTML or PDF documents for the January 14 or March 4, 2026 releases.

issues_found: Source is a navigation page (source_type: navigation_page) - per Rule 5, this must be rejected
No actual Beige Book content present - only a landing page describing what the Beige Book is and listing publication dates
source.url is blank in source metadata - no link to actual Beige Book content
event_type is 'unknown' - correctly identified as problematic, but the real issue is this is not archiveable source material at all
No district-level economic commentary or data to support any macro catalyst analysis
The 'Last Update: March 04, 2026' in source body contradicts the record's claim that 'source.published_at is blank' - the date is present in the page content

notes: Non-archiveable: This source is a Federal Reserve website navigation page for the Beige Book, not the actual report content. The page merely describes what the Beige Book is and lists publication dates. No district-level economic commentary, data, or analysis is present. The actual Beige Book releases (January 14 and March 4, 2026) would need to be sourced separately. SOURCE METADATA TARGET was 'macro catalysts' but the source content does not contain macro data.
